observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management:
In preparation for the implementation of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances:
To direct the GSO to update the property cards and all its records pertaining to the PPE accounts
To direct the Accountant to:
Ensure complete preparation and updating of records putting into consideration the properties being demolished, disposed, transfer to other agencies, pending request relief and others which were not recorded yet both in the books of accounts and property ledger cards.
Conduct reconciliation of property ledger balances and the balances of each PPE account in the general ledger/financial statements.
To implement COA Circular 2020-06, dated January 31, 2020, or the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, to be able to come up with reliable balances thereof that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability. In line with this, an Inventory Committee, specifically for the purpose of one-time cleansing, should be created. This committee should be different from the Inventory Committee recommended in Finding No. 3, as the latter is responsible for the regular physical count of the agency.
That after the cut-off date set by the Inventory Committee in the implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts, both the GSO and Accounting shall ensure the proper accounting and reporting procedures for all acquisition/receipt/issue/transfer/disposal of PPEs pursuant to the Accounting Manuals of Local Government Units and other relevant laws, rules, and regulations, which shall be strictly followed.
Regardless of the period of implementation of the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances,
To supervise regular conduct of physical inventory of all PPEs at least once a year and shall prepare and submit the RPCPPE to the GSO so that the necessary action can be effected, to the Municipal Accounting Office for subsequent reconciliation with the books of accounts, and to the audit team.
To direct the Accountant to ensure all PPEs owned by the LGU, except for non-depreciable ones, are provided with the appropriate allowances for depreciation.
To direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to work hand in hand and establish strong internal control on the management of PPEs.
Management Comment:
The management commented to create the required Inventory Committee and to adhere to the recommendations.

We recommended to
The Municipal Mayor
to issue an executive order to create an inventory committee, which will plan and strategize the conduct of physical inventories.
Provide a storeroom of supplies and materials, as it will aid proper monitoring of the same.
The Inventory Committee
to conduct a physical count on all inventory items and submit the resulting RPCI and RPCSP to the GSO so that the necessary action can be effected, to the Municipal Accounting Office for subsequent reconciliation with the books of accounts, and to the audit team, on a semestral basis.
The construction materials in different warehouses should be prioritized so that an appropriate adjusting entry will be drawn by the Municipal Accounting Office.
The GSO
to maintain stock cards and record the issuance in the stock cards based on the RIS and the SSMI.
Submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accounting Office to support the recording of the issuance of inventory items.
Maintain the RegSPI for proper monitoring of issued semi-expendable properties.
The Municipal Accountant
to draw the necessary adjusting journal entries to arrive at the correct balance of the various inventory and expense accounts;
Consistently apply the Perpetual Inventory Method in the recording of supplies and inventories and to recognize expense only upon issuance thereof to end-user supported with the submission of necessary supporting documents such as SSMI, supported with RIS, from the GSO.
The Municipal Accountant and GSO are to reconcile their respective records on inventories.
Henceforth, ensure continuity of application of all the above recommendations in order to establish a strong internal control over management of supplies and materials.
Management Comment:
Management admitted to the lack of internal control and mentioned that they will try their best to improve the current practice of the LGU. The monitoring of the construction materials observed in the warehouse will be prioritized for the implementation of internal control.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the lacking documents marked “X” and remarked in Annex G.
Submit justifications for:
the difference in the number of participants in the attendance sheet compared to the number of meals/snacks procured, as remarked in Annex G.
Not subjecting various procurements of meals and snacks to public bidding
Henceforth,
Ensure that attendance sheets, activity designs, canvass, and documents when applicable, attached to the disbursement vouchers for meals and snacks, are complete, reliable, and satisfactory.
Other supporting documents evidencing actual conduct of the event should also be attached, such as, but not limited to, photos of the events and minutes of meetings.
Include justification for service of breakfast and dinner, when applicable.
Ensure budgetary requirements for meals and snacks in the activity designs have a breakdown of what type of meal/snacks to be ordered, the budget price for each pax, as well as the total amount.
Procurements for meals and snacks should be consolidated on a quarterly basis and should be subjected to public bidding.
Meals and snacks purchased related to the conduct of trainings should be debited to training expenses.
Prudence should be observed in the procurement of meals and snacks.
Management Comment:
Management commented to look into the transactions and to improve the practice of the LGU one at a time.

We recommended to the management to:
Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents of the JEVs mentioned in Annex H to complete post-audit and verification.
Henceforth, ensure that supporting documents attached will render enough explanation and information to the audit team to aid post-audit and verification of each of the transactions and will warrant the necessity, validity, propriety, and regularity of the transactions of the LGU.
Management Comment:
Management commented to indicate breakdowns and or explanations to all intra-agency transfers starting in CY 2025.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit immediately to the audit team the enumerated lacking documents to complete our post audit. Secure documents from the LMP-Bukidnon Chapter regarding the breakdown of paid registration fees to accurately account for the same, including the pertinent documents necessary for the proper liquidation thereof. Non-submission may warrant the issuance of an audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit a duly signed JEV No. 100-2024-10-04574, and henceforth:
Ensure that payments for “registration fees” for purposes directly related to travel expenses should be treated as cash advances, subject to proper documentation and liquidation; and
Ensure that expenses related to foreign travel are recorded to the Travelling Expenses – Foreign account.
Ensure that every disbursement of government funds is safeguarded against illegal or improper disposition and that the funds are utilized efficiently, economically, and effectively.
Management Comment:
Management has already provided some of the required documents and promised to submit other required documents as soon as they are available.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to immediately stop the practice of granting honoraria to officials and employees other than those authorized under by law.
Management Comment:
Management commented on being unaware that the grant is bereft of legal basis but agreed on the observation. They mentioned to immediately stop granting the honoraria.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the HRMO to:
Correct and update the individual leave cards, taking into consideration the above observations, particularly on the correction of charges from SL credits that are supposed to be charged from VL credits and the excessive charges from SL credits as discussed in finding “A” above and in consideration of the discussions in Audit Finding No. 9 of this audit report. Employees with negative balances in VL after correction, if any, should not be allowed to avail VL until its negative balance will eventually accumulate VL credits.
Submit to the audit team a duly certified summary list of leave credits of all officials and employees as of December 31, 2024.
For employees having negative leave balances after correction, disapprove succeeding applications for monetizations. Only allow monetizations once VL credits reach at least 15.
submit NOSA as of the date of application of monetization or equivalent certification indicating the current monthly salary of the employee.
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents noted in Annex I. For documents lacking approval, resubmit such documents complete with the required signatories or a certification of the receipt of payment, whichever is applicable.
Henceforth, the HR should see to it to strictly abide by the requirements on the Omnibus Rules on Leave regarding the approval of applications for monetization of the officials and employees of the LGU, and the Municipal Accountant should ensure completeness of the necessary supporting documents, including either the NOSA or the equivalent certification indicating the current monthly salary of the employee.
Management Comment:
The HRMO acknowledged that the findings were indeed occurring in the LGU and committed to adhering to the recommendations.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
to direct the Human Resource Management Officer to prepare corrected ledger cards for each employee as of December 2024 with consideration to the observations discussed above.
Thereafter, submit certified true copies of individual leave cards of all regular and casual employees to the audit team to complete the post-audit of CY 2024 payrolls on or before June 30, 2025.
Furthermore, the HRMO to submit justification for allowing the non-filing of leave applications for absences.
Henceforth, we recommended to the HRMO to strictly abide by the requirements on the Omnibus Rules on Leave related to the monitoring and updating of leave credits of each of the employees, such as but not limited to regular updating of leave cards; recording of earned leaves, as well as the converted credits of late and undertimes, on a monthly basis; deduction of forfeited unused mandatory/forced leave at the end of the year; and ensuring correct balances of leave cards at all times.
Management Comment:
Management committed to adhering to the recommendations.

We recommended to the management to direct the HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to:
submit copies of the lacking DTRs in Annex K.
review copies of DTRs of all employees from January to December 2024 and ensure submission of complete travel documents for all entries on travel and approved applications for leave.
take note of the DTRs with superimpositions during the review of the DTRs and submit reprinted copies of those DTRs directly from the biometric machine, certified correct by the HRMO.
submit justification for the inconsistent filing of application for leave of the employees.
Prepare corrected individual leave cards of employees as recommended in Observation No. 9 and exclude deductions of unauthorized leaves.
Compute the equivalent amount paid to employees related to unauthorized leaves and require refunds of the same.
Avoid payment of unauthorized leaves and the practice of deduction of unauthorized leave from individual leave credits, as these should be deducted in the succeeding payment of salary.
Submit justification of allowing handwritten entries without approved request for manual entries, allowing some days in the DTR to be left blank, allowing incomplete logs of AM in, AM out, PM in, and PM out fields, the use of correction tapes, the presence of superimpositions, allowing entirely handwritten DTRs without justifications, allowing employees not to use biometric machines, and justifications on the detachments of supporting documents in the DTRs submitted by the employees.
Submit certified true copies of travel orders not yet submitted to the audit team.
Non-submission of the above on or before June 30, 2025, warrants issuances of Notice(s) of suspensions or disallowances, as the case may be.
Henceforth, direct the HRMO and Municipal Accountant, as the case may be, to:
Maintain a logbook of received Daily Time Records from employees.
Require attachments of requests for manual entries duly approved both by the supervisor/department head and the HRMO for handwritten time logs.
Ensure complete attachments and submission to the audit team of all supporting documents necessary to support all the entries in the DTRs.
Attach certification from the HRMO that absences, late arrivals, and undertimes were already posted/charged to their respective leave credits.
After review of submitted DTRs, ensure deduction of the equivalent amount of all unauthorized leaves paid.
Ensure all fields (AM In, AM Out, PM In, PM Out, as well as fields of overtimes, if any) were completely filled out.
Do not allow superimpositions and erasures, particularly on the use of correction tapes.
Biometric time logs should be utilized as much as possible.
Management Comment:
Management is committed to implementing the audit recommendations and improving their practices, particularly those that impact the credibility of the daily time records.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to:
Reconcile records of the “Due to GSIS”, “Due to HDMF”, “Due to PHIC” and “Due to BIR” accounts to identify the affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies.
Timely update records of the employees to the system/records in the GSIS, HDMF, PHIC, and BIR.
Ensure intact monthly remittances of withheld taxes, contributions, and loan repayments within the prescribed period by strengthening the monitoring of withholding and deductions from employees, suppliers and contractors and cross-checking the records of withholding and the lists for remittance, particularly for employees with changes in the rate/amount of deduction/withholding during the month.
Conduct regular reconciliation of records, especially when there are unremitted balances in the “Due to GSIS”, “Due to HDMF”, “Due to PHIC”, and “Due to BIR” that do not relate to the latest month of withholding/deduction.
Management Comment:
Management committed to conduct reconciliations of the accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Management to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to effect fund transfers for the remittance of the special accounts, SEF, and TF before the GF Proper would remit the mandatory deductions to the respective government agencies. We further recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office include a breakdown/detailed explanation for each of the cash transfers stated in Annex N and do the same for the future cash transfers of the LGU.
Management Comment:
Management committed to adhering to the requirement of fund transfer of cash to the General Fund Proper prior to the remittance of the mandatory deductions.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the remittance of the unutilized collection from the sale of bid documents in CY 2024, totaling ₱470,818.00, to the General Fund.
Henceforth, record the proceeds from the sale of bid documents in the "Trust Liabilities" account and remit to the General Fund the unutilized collection from the sale of bid documents at the end of the year.
Management Comment:
The management commented that they were not aware of the said rule, however, in compliance to the recommendation, they already transferred the unutilized collection from the sale of bid documents as of December 31, 2024 in the amount of P470,818.00 and committed to abide to the said rule in the succeeding years.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Acting Municipal Mayor to direct all officials involved in the processing to solidarily pay the full amount of P200,000.00 on or before April 2025. Otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.
Management Comment:
The management promised to direct the persons’ responsible to refund the P200,000.00.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to submit on time the check and cash disbursements, cash receipts, and general journal transactions, including their supporting documents, to the Office of the Auditor.

We further recommended to the management to initiate the necessary administrative and/or criminal action in case of unjustified failure to effect compliance with the foregoing requirements or to cause the automatic suspension of the payment of salary and other emoluments until the concerned official or personnel shall have complied therewith.
observations.reason: The submission of financial reports in CY 2024 is still delayed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Henceforth, assign personnel to prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels. The report should detail the trips of individual drivers in chronological order.
Provide justification for the non-submission of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of the MEO and MACCO.
Henceforth, require all offices with fuel consumption for the month to submit a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor.
Henceforth, direct concerned personnel to review the reports thoroughly to ensure its accuracy.
Ensure that all required data in the reports are properly accomplished, and the formulas used in the report are accurate. Thereafter, significant excesses should be investigated by the management to prevent wasteful, excessive, and unnecessary expenditures of fuel.
observations.reason: All recommendations not yet complied

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Direct the concerned officials to submit the lacking certifications.
Direct the HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to recompute the payment of TA for CY 2023 with considerations to the submitted certifications by the concerned officials.  Overpayments, if there are any, should be therefore be restituted to the funds of the LGU. Also, submit to the audit team copy of the working paper of the recomputation and the complete supporting documents shall be thereto attached.
Henceforth, to choose one from the following:
Assign vehicle to the head of office but not to pay the commutable TA;
Pay the commutable TA but will not assign vehicle to the head of office; or
Pay the commutable TA but with corresponding deduction if the head of office used a government vehicle; and
Henceforth, ensure that the payment for RATA is supported with complete prescribed documents.
observations.reason: Still some officials not deducted Transportation allowance despite the use of vehicle

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
determine the total accountability of the AOs (collectors, disbursing officers, and municipal treasurer) using their respective records of collections and disbursements as the basis for their fidelity bonds,
ensure that all AOs are properly and adequately bonded with reference to the determined total accountability, and
ensure that the disbursing officer is not granted cash advances that exceed the maximum cash accountability of their fidelity bonds.
observations.reason: But DO has still been granted cash advances that exceed his maximum accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Instruct all collectors of the following:
Refrain from using the collections for personal purposes,
RCDs should be prepared at the close of each business day, reporting and remitting all collections during the day, and
Ensure the accuracy of the collections and accountable forms reported in the RCD and to avoid any erasures and alterations in the RCD to maintain its integrity.
Instruct the LO to verify all collections remitted by the collectors and the balances of accountable forms reported in the RCD.
Consider relieving some tasks from the LO, allowing her to focus on her role as a liquidating officer, particularly the verification of all collections and accountable forms’ balances of the collectors.
Stop allowing officials of the municipality use the collections for their personal purposes.
Ensure that all collections are deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day.
observations.reason: All recommendation not yet fully adhered to in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct all the accountable officers, particularly the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the prescribed format for the cashbooks and its instructions thereof.

We further recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to do the following:

Immediately commence the creation of a report number for each RCD of all AOs,
Require all AOs to include the report number of RCDs in the cashbook, 
The recording of collections and deposits should be per RCD,
Ensure the daily updating and footing of the cash in treasury cashbook, including the running balances.
observations.reason: Various deposits were not per RCD

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Submit additional documentation to support the validity of the time extensions granted, specifically the following:
Construction of Road at the Proposed National Highway:
Three (3) Evaluation Reports by the management, each corresponding to the suspension orders, establishing the need for the suspension and time extension, with sufficient details and computation on how the number of days granted was derived
Copy of the approved original PERT/CPM Network Diagram
Copy of the approved revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram
PAGASA weather certification
List of affected property boundaries, duly certified by the Municipal Engineer
Construction of Hospital:
Evaluation Report by the agency concerned establishing the need for suspension or time extension
Copy of the approved original PERT/CPM Network Diagram
Copy of the approved revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram
Other additional document indicating the specific project causing the suspension of work, and when the project was finally completed

If the above documents cannot be submitted to support the validity of the time extensions granted, re-compute the liquidated damages incorporating the invalid time extension granted and collect the same from the contractor.

Minimize/avoid further delays in project implementation by carrying out the necessary preliminary engineering study and the detailed engineering activities to ensure that problems such as road right of way are properly addressed prior to project implementation. Moreover, henceforth, ensure that no award of contract for Infrastructure Projects shall be made unless the detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs, for the project have been sufficiently carried out and duly approved in accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the HoPE.
observations.reason: No submission of required additional documents; No adjustment made for liquidated Damages

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the following documents to complete our post-audit of JEV No. 100-2023-05-01970:
Approved documents containing the number and complete technical specifications of the equipment to be rented (e.g., brand name, make/country of origin, hp, model, piston displacement, etc.)
Approved detailed breakdown of the approved budget for the contract and the contract amount showing in sufficient detail its derivation
Agency evaluation of equipment utilization
Pertinent data of area of operation.
observations.reason: No Submission of required Documents

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the local government personnel who received the MDRRMC honoraria to immediately refund the amount received in CY 2023.
Henceforth, discontinue the grant of honoraria to the members of the MDRRMC who are government personnel.
observations.reason: No refund has yet been made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to take the following steps to ensure the accurate and fair presentation of the financial statements of the LGU:

Henceforth, ensure proper classification of all travel, training, and membership dues disbursements in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs,
Ensure that all supporting documents, such as official receipts, are complete and attached to the disbursement voucher or liquidation report, and
Require the officials/employees to submit the lacking official receipts to support their claims, otherwise, refund the same.

We recommended to the Management to:
Establish a clear and consistent set of policies and procedures for the grant of financial assistance, including the amount to be disbursed for each specific case and criteria for eligibility of recipients, to ensure that each disbursement made will be fair and equitable, and 
Maintain detailed records of the recipients to avoid duplicate claims and payments for the same beneficiary, which can conserve the limited funds of the LGU and help more constituents in need.
observations.reason: Initial planning has been conducted, but the set of policies and procedures agreed upon has yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a)	Ensure that all staff involved in the disbursement process are knowledgeable on the guidelines provided by the BIR in RMC No. 23-2007, particularly on the different levels of withholding taxes that apply to different types of suppliers and purchases,
b)	Implement a system of checks and controls to ensure that all payments to suppliers are correctly calculated and accurately reflect the withholding taxes,
c)	Review past disbursements (you may refer to the attached Annex F of this report), to identify and correct any under-withholding, and promptly remit the required amount to the national government.
observations.reason: There are inaccurate computations found in CY 2023 transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 by taking the following steps:
a)	Facilitate the preparation and submission of photocopies of previous payrolls for the same projects, SLs, inspection reports for completed projects, and the submission of certification of payment received for unsigned payrolls to complete the post-audit of the disbursements,
b)	See to it that all subsequent labor disbursements for construction are supported with complete documentation; in addition to the usual supporting documents prepared, include inspection reports for completed projects, or any equivalent document, copies of previous payrolls for payments of labor related to the same project, and Job Order Appointments, and
c)	Ensure that for subsequent disbursements, certifications in the payrolls are duly signed and all payees have signed received.
observations.reason: No Submission of the noted lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
a.	Instruct the PMC to conduct an immediate review of the projects under continuing appropriations that are still ongoing or pending; evaluate whether the projects may still be continued or should already be cancelled and appropriated for new projects;
b.	Direct concerned offices to coordinate in project monitoring and implementation to ensure that funds are utilized efficiently and effectively; and
c.	Reinforce LGU’s monitoring and evaluation system, especially for development projects, by ensuring that the PMC is properly organized and functional. The PMC should closely monitor the status of each project and proactively seek feedback from the concerned offices for reasons for delays in completion or implementation, and coordinate with the concerned offices to resolve issues encountered. The PMC should also monitor the balances of appropriations for completed projects, so that the remaining funds may be reappropriated for relevant development projects for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.reason:
LGU
is still in the process of reviewing previous projects

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation to the management to perform the following procedures:
1)The Sangguniang Bayan to convene immediately to amend/revise the standing ordinance by clearly identifying PBCC as a local economic enterprise of the municipality. 
2)The Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect adjustment relative to the transfer of affected accounts from Trust Fund to General Fund, duly supported with working papers. Likewise, compute for the surplus generated by PBCC from date of inception up to CY 2021, subject for subsequent legislative enactment;
3)The Municipal Budget Officer to facilitate/coordinate/prepare the budget proposals on such declared surplus subject for legislative review and authorization. Moreover, include, henceforth, the budget of PBCC in the “Annual Operating Budget of Local Economic Enterprise(s)” document, as a pertinent document to be attached to Annual Budget. The Sangguniang Bayan to hastily review and authorize the said budget document/s;
4)The management to submit the foregoing mentioned documents to the audit team for our further evaluation; and,
5)Stop, henceforth, the improper practice of treating subsidies and other receipts favorable to PBCC as funds held in trust under Trust Fund. Instead, this should be treated as receipts to the general fund to be recorded to the appropriate income/trust accounts.

In addition, we recommended to the management to submit a valid justification for the non-compliance of the prior year’s recommendation and to exercise extraordinary caution in processing disbursements of PBCC prior the required legislative actions in order not to expose accountable officials to any “illegal” disbursements without the covering appropriations.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the entry for such disbursed transaction which is subject for liquidation by debiting Advances to Officers and Employees and crediting the Prior Period Adjustment accounts. Subsequently, required the accountable SK officials to immediately submit to the audit team the related liquidation documents thereof. Ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documentation as required by COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the BAC, to submit the noted lacking supporting documents to the audit team for post-audit and further evaluation. Likewise, submit valid justification and/or resubmit documents that would at least explain/suffice the other deficiencies noted. Henceforth, meticulously and sufficiently support disbursement vouchers with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to the audit team.

Further, enforced the collection of these liquidated damages (LD) from the identified supplier in default. Furthermore, in case similar situation may arise, the Municipal Accounting Office to be strict in imposing LD by deducting the computed amount against the supplier’s claim.

Moreover, implement measures to strictly monitor the deliveries of procured items vis-à-vis the agreed contract duration. The BAC, upon post-qualification process, to ensure that the awarded supplier can/will faithfully comply with the terms and conditions of the contract, especially on the scheduled date of delivery. Impose other appropriate sanctions against the erring supplier, such as the enforcement of liquidated damages, termination/rescission of contract, forfeiture of posted performance security, and availment of blacklisting procedure.
observations.reason: Documents that would at least explain the deficiencies noted not submitted.

1) contractor’s request,
2) additional supporting documents for time extensions,
3) Contractor’s SWA.

Specifically, proper coordination among offices involved in the processing of these claims should be made, especially the Municipal Engineering Office, BAC and the Municipal Accounting Office. The latter office should meticulously review and ensure that these pertinent documents are completely attached to DVs prior to disbursement.

observations.reason: Incomplete submission of required supporting documents,

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to submit immediately to the audit team the aforementioned lacking documents to complete our evaluation and, henceforth, attach these relevant evidential documents for current disbursements. Likewise, the management to set the authorized monthly honorarium per official thru the issuance of a legislative resolution.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation to the management to require the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to record, henceforth, in the books of accounts the procured inventories and equipment as debit to appropriate inventory and equipment accounts and not directly to the liability account. Drop the distributed inventories in the books of accounts based on RSMI, supported with RIS. Further, required the MAO, in coordination with the MDRRMO and MHO to submit immediately the required RIS, RSMI, PAR and inventory report to the audit team for our further evaluation. Prior to submission, ensure that the total quantity of the procured items is reconciled with the total quantity issued/distributed, so as not to warrant issuance of disallowance on discrepancies noted, if there is any.

Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the procured unrecorded equipment amounting to P749,999.64. Submit to the audit team the said JEV for our review and evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC Chairman and General Services Officer, to submit the noted lacking supporting documents to the audit team for post-audit, in order to conclude the appropriateness, legality and validity of the disbursed transactions. 

Moreover, required the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, to meticulously and sufficiently support disbursement vouchers with required pertinent documents prior to payment and submission to audit team.
observations.reason: Incomplete submission of the required documents

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the differences between the accounting records and the Consolidated Quarterly Report on Government Project/Activities to ensure that projects already completed should no longer be recorded under the CIP accounts. Moreover, implement an effective monitoring and evaluation control on the status of implementation of infrastructure and other projects by organizing a Local Project Monitoring Committee pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circulars No. 2004-78 and 2019-188 dated 22 June 2004 and 14 November 2019, respectively.

Required the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the relevant documents such as Certificate of Completion and Inspection Report, and other pertinent documents as bases of the latter to reclassify the CIP account to its specific PPE account.

Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to conduct detailed analysis and effect necessary adjusting entries relative to the un-reclassified accounts and the affected accumulated depreciations, if warranted.
observations.reason: No reconciliation of records made nor adjustments in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
The honorable Mayor to create an Inventory Committee who will be responsible in the conduct of the physical inventory of the property, plant and equipment, and thereafter require the conduct of physical count and submission of the report annually. 

Required the GSO to maintain property cards per category of property, plant and equipment and require the accounting office to recognized depreciation expense to depreciable items in the PPE account.
observations.reason: Non-conduct of actual physical inventory in CY 2024

observations.recommendation:
Required the Municipal Assessor to follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality) at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements information on these real properties with DAR MOA agreement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to submit on time the check and cash disbursements, cash receipts, and general journal transactions, including their supporting documents, to the Office of the Auditor.

We further recommended to the management to initiate the necessary administrative and/or criminal action in case of unjustified failure to effect compliance with the foregoing requirements or to cause the automatic suspension of the payment of salary and other emoluments until the concerned official or personnel shall have complied therewith.

We recommended to the management to:
Henceforth, assign personnel to prepare a Monthly Report of Official Travels. The report should detail the trips of individual drivers in chronological order.
Provide justification for the non-submission of Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of the MEO and MACCO.
Henceforth, require all offices with fuel consumption for the month to submit a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor.
Henceforth, direct concerned personnel to review the reports thoroughly to ensure its accuracy.
Ensure that all required data in the reports are properly accomplished, and the formulas used in the report are accurate. Thereafter, significant excesses should be investigated by the management to prevent wasteful, excessive, and unnecessary expenditures of fuel.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the concerned officials to submit the lacking certifications.
Direct the HRMO and the Municipal Accountant to recompute the payment of TA for CY 2023 with considerations to the submitted certifications by the concerned officials.  Overpayments, if there are any, should be therefore be restituted to the funds of the LGU. Also, submit to the audit team copy of the working paper of the recomputation and the complete supporting documents shall be thereto attached.
Henceforth, to choose one from the following:
Assign vehicle to the head of office but not to pay the commutable TA;
Pay the commutable TA but will not assign vehicle to the head of office; or
Pay the commutable TA but with corresponding deduction if the head of office used a government vehicle; and
Henceforth, ensure that the payment for RATA is supported with complete prescribed documents.

We recommended to the management to:
determine the total accountability of the AOs (collectors, disbursing officers, and municipal treasurer) using their respective records of collections and disbursements as the basis for their fidelity bonds,
ensure that all AOs are properly and adequately bonded with reference to the determined total accountability, and
ensure that the disbursing officer is not granted cash advances that exceed the maximum cash accountability of their fidelity bonds.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Instruct all collectors of the following:
Refrain from using the collections for personal purposes,
RCDs should be prepared at the close of each business day, reporting and remitting all collections during the day, and
Ensure the accuracy of the collections and accountable forms reported in the RCD and to avoid any erasures and alterations in the RCD to maintain its integrity.
Instruct the LO to verify all collections remitted by the collectors and the balances of accountable forms reported in the RCD.
Consider relieving some tasks from the LO, allowing her to focus on her role as a liquidating officer, particularly the verification of all collections and accountable forms’ balances of the collectors.
Stop allowing officials of the municipality use the collections for their personal purposes.
Ensure that all collections are deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended to the management to direct all the accountable officers, particularly the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the prescribed format for the cashbooks and its instructions thereof.

We further recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to do the following:
Immediately commence the creation of a report number for each RCD of all AOs,
Require all AOs to include the report number of RCDs in the cashbook,
The recording of collections and deposits should be per RCD,
Ensure the daily updating and footing of the cash in treasury cashbook, including the running balances.

We recommended to the management to:
Submit additional documentation to support the validity of the time extensions granted, specifically the following:
Construction of Road at the Proposed National Highway:
Three (3) Evaluation Reports by the management, each corresponding to the suspension orders, establishing the need for the suspension and time extension, with sufficient details and computation on how the number of days granted was derived
Copy of the approved original PERT/CPM Network Diagram
Copy of the approved revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram
PAGASA weather certification
List of affected property boundaries, duly certified by the Municipal Engineer
Construction of Hospital:
Evaluation Report by the agency concerned establishing the need for suspension or time extension
Copy of the approved original PERT/CPM Network Diagram
Copy of the approved revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram
Other additional document indicating the specific project causing the suspension of work, and when the project was finally completed
If the above documents cannot be submitted to support the validity of the time extensions granted, re-compute the liquidated damages incorporating the invalid time extension granted and collect the same from the contractor.
Minimize/avoid further delays in project implementation by carrying out the necessary preliminary engineering study and the detailed engineering activities to ensure that problems such as road right of way are properly addressed prior to project implementation. Moreover, henceforth, ensure that no award of contract for Infrastructure Projects shall be made unless the detailed engineering investigations, surveys and designs, for the project have been sufficiently carried out and duly approved in accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the HoPE.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the following documents to complete our post audit of JEV No. 100-2023-05-01970:
Approved documents containing the number and complete technical specifications of the equipment to be rented (e.g., brand name, make/country of origin, hp, model, piston displacement, etc.)
Approved detailed breakdown of the approved budget for the contract and the contract amount showing in sufficient detail its derivation
Agency evaluation of equipment utilization
Pertinent data of area of operation.

We recommended to the management to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the local government personnel who received the MDRRMC honoraria to immediately refund the amount received in CY 2023.
Henceforth, discontinue the grant of honoraria to the members of the MDRRMC who are government personnel.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to take the following steps to ensure the accurate and fair presentation of the financial statements of the LGU:

Henceforth, ensure proper classification of all travel, training, and membership dues disbursements in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs,
Ensure that all supporting documents, such as official receipts, are complete and attached to the disbursement voucher or liquidation report, and
Require the officials/employees to submit the lacking official receipts to support their claims, otherwise, refund the same.

observations.recommendation:

Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the above-mentioned transactions and to ensure that succeeding disbursements of the same nature are recorded in the proper accounts, and the
Municipal Engineer to prepare/submit inspection reports for the above-mentioned projects to complete the post-audit of the transactions and to ensure that subsequent disbursements are supported with inspection reports or equivalent documentation.

We recommended to the management to direct following:
the MTO to submit the RCI and the original cancelled checks together with the DVs and its supporting documents to the MACCO, and
the MACCO to forward the RCI and original cancelled checks together with the DVs and supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, in accordance with the procedure provided in the NGAS Manual for LGUs, to ensure that every check issuance is properly and completely recorded and accounted for.

We recommended to the Management to:
Establish a clear and consistent set of policies and procedures for the grant of financial assistance, including the amount to be disbursed for each specific case and criteria for eligibility of recipients, to ensure that each disbursement made will be fair and equitable, and 
Maintain detailed records of the recipients to avoid duplicate claims and payments for the same beneficiary, which can conserve the limited funds of the LGU and help more constituents in need.
observations.reason: Initial planning has been conducted, but the set of policies and procedures agreed upon has yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Ensure that all staff involved in the disbursement process are knowledgeable on the guidelines provided by the BIR in RMC No. 23-2007, particularly on the different levels of withholding taxes that apply to different types of suppliers and purchases,
b) Implement a system of checks and controls to ensure that all payments to suppliers are correctly calculated and accurately reflect the withholding taxes,
c) Review past disbursements (you may refer to the attached Annex F of this report), to identify and correct any under-withholding, and promptly remit the required amount to the national government.
observations.reason: There are inaccurate computations found in CY 2023 transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
a) instruct its officials and employees of the required supporting documents for transportation expenses, as provided in COA Circular 2021-001,
b) direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required supporting documents are attached to every transportation claim made, and to
c) discontinue the practice of providing a taxi allowance for travels and only allow disbursements for actual transportation expenses made.

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 by taking the following steps:
a) Facilitate the preparation and submission of photocopies of previous payrolls for the same projects, SLs, inspection reports for completed projects, and the submission of certification of payment received for unsigned payrolls to complete the post-audit of the disbursements,
b) See to it that all subsequent labor disbursements for construction are supported with complete documentation; in addition to the usual supporting documents prepared, include inspection reports for completed projects, or any equivalent document, copies of previous payrolls for payments of labor related to the same project, and Job Order Appointments, and
c) Ensure that for subsequent disbursements, certifications in the payrolls are duly signed and all payees have signed received.
observations.reason: No submission of the noted lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
a. Instruct the PMC to conduct an immediate review of the projects under continuing appropriations that are still ongoing or pending; evaluate whether the projects may still be continued or should already be cancelled and appropriated for new projects;
b. Direct concerned offices to coordinate in project monitoring and implementation to ensure that funds are utilized efficiently and effectively; and
c. Reinforce LGU’s monitoring and evaluation system, especially for development projects, by ensuring that the PMC is properly organized and functional. The PMC should closely monitor the status of each project and proactively seek feedback from the concerned offices for reasons for delays in completion or implementation, and coordinate with the concerned offices to resolve issues encountered. The PMC should also monitor the balances of appropriations for completed projects, so that the remaining funds may be reappropriated for relevant development projects for the benefit of the constituents.
observations.reason: LGU is still in the process of reviewing previous projects and still has to improve its monitoring on each of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation to the management to perform the following procedures:
1) The Sangguniang Bayan to convene immediately to amend/revise the standing ordinance by clearly identifying PBCC as a local economic enterprise of the municipality. 
2) The Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to effect adjustment relative to the transfer of affected accounts from Trust Fund to General Fund, duly supported with working papers. Likewise, compute for the surplus generated by PBCC from date of inception up to CY 2021, subject for subsequent legislative enactment;
3) The Municipal Budget Officer to facilitate/coordinate/prepare the budget proposals on such declared surplus subject for legislative review and authorization. Moreover, include, henceforth, the budget of PBCC in the “Annual Operating Budget of Local Economic Enterprise(s)” document, as a pertinent document to be attached to Annual Budget. The Sangguniang Bayan to hastily review and authorize the said budget document/s;
4) The management to submit the foregoing mentioned documents to the audit team for our further evaluation; and,
5) Stop, henceforth, the improper practice of treating subsidies and other receipts favorable to PBCC as funds held in trust under Trust Fund. Instead, this should be treated as receipts to the general fund to be recorded to the appropriate income/trust accounts.

In addition, we recommended to the management to submit a valid justification for the non-compliance of the prior year’s recommendation and to exercise extraordinary caution in processing disbursements of PBCC prior the required legislative actions in order not to expose accountable officials to any “illegal” disbursements without the covering appropriations.

observations.recommendation:

Submit to this audit team a copy thereof for our evaluation. Also, directed the same to maintain lapsing schedule as basis for the computation of depreciation of every item during the year. Likewise, subsequent purchases of items with less than P15,000.00 should be recorded as expense once issued. 

Furthermore, required the Municipal Budget Officer to consider budgeted items as capital outlays only once the P15,000 threshold and such other criteria for the recognition of PPE are met.

Moreover, the management to initiate the creation of Inventory Committee for the conduct of physical count of all properties and inventories owned by the LGU in which such annual mandatory procedure was unprioritized for several years already, in order to enhance reliability of account balances presented in the financial statement by observing the guidelines and procedures set under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition and disposition of PPE items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the municipality.

observations.reason: The management failed to continue the annual physical count initiated in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, henceforth, discontinue the grant of honoraria to BAC and TWG members sourced out from proceeds from liquidated damages. Adhere strictly to Section 3.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007 to avoid audit disallowance of transactions. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant to properly account the proceeds from liquidated damages as Miscellaneous Income and not as Other Payables, in order to enhance the reliability and accuracy of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to review the proper accounting treatment of recording the salaries and wages through ATM payment. Stop the current improper practice which entails additional and unnecessary preparation, review, recognition and submission of liquidation reports and journal entry vouchers, not to mention the unwarranted extra workloads to be rendered by the Municipal Accounting Office personnel.

We recommended that management discontinue the grant of livelihood program through cash loan scheme to pre-selected barangay volunteers and leaders. The Executive and Legislative Department to consider implementing other social welfare programs and other basic services and facilities mandated by the Local Government Code, which are essential to benefit and promote the general welfare of its constituents and not just a selected few individuals. 

For those already granted with loan, close monitoring should be conducted periodically by the Municipal Livelihood Committee. Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in demanding automatic full refund of loan that was diverted to other purpose, other than what was agreed in the Memorandum of Agreement and in strict adherence also to the implementing guidelines of the said program. 

Also, discontinue the improper use of Donation budgetary account title for loan-related program of the LGU.

Moreover, the Municipal Livelihood Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the noted difference in their record balances to enhance reliability of Loans Receivable account balance reflected in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive be prudent and judicious in exercising his fiscal responsibility, along with the Accountant and Treasurer, by strictly adhering to the proper disbursement process. Revisit and reevaluate the current disbursement procedures of the agency. Moreover, implement a good internal control system to prevent incurrence of such improper practice of issuing checks for disbursement vouchers with inadequate signatures of proper officials.

We recommended that management require the Head of Human Resource Management Unit (HRMU) and the Municipal Accountant to:

a.) Submit immediately to COA the following required documents to support the disbursed transactions, in which failure to do so may warrant audit suspension or disallowance, as the case may be:
     1) Certifications from  the HRMU Head justifying the necessity to grant the monetization using the sick leave credit balances, supported with valid justifications from those who requested the monetization; 
2.) Duly certified and approved Application for Leave forms for those already paid but lacked the certification from HRMU Head and approval from the LCE; and,
3.) Leave card duly certified by the HRMU Head for those who have requested such monetization.
b.) Update all leave cards of officials and employees and assure that every request for monetization must be posted to leave cards prudently and conscientiously; 
c.) Discontinue the grant of monetization of leave credits to top government officials without first giving preference/priority to those employees holding positions below salary grade 19; and,
d.) Limit the grant of monetization to once a year only and adhere strictly to other applicable rules and regulations governing the monetization of leave credits of government officials and employees.

Henceforth, reappropriate funds to other more beneficial priority projects and programs for the general welfare of its constituents.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant requested the Sangguniang Bayan to effect an Ordinance reverting some unclaimed payables amounting to P2.001 Million.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the Municipal Accountant to reissue demand letters and exhaust all possible remedies to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of these accounts. Comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables and cash advances accounts to the Commission on Audit. Eliminate reciprocal/intra-agency accounts during consolidation of financial statements, in order to have a fair presentation of affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Sent demand letters. Abode recommendations to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements and to effect adherence to PPSAS guidelines
observations.reason: On-going verification

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant and municipal budget officer to be more discreet in the grant of BAC honoraria in accordance with the guidelines set by the DBM. Stop the practice of granting BAC honoraria from the general fund which is bereft of legal basis.
observations.managementAction: The management abode on the recommendation to secure proper adherence to be effected at all times.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to observe a judicious and frugal measure on its disbursements relative to meals, snacks and other commodities to be served for guests and employees during visits, meetings and some other activities conducted by the Municipality. Allocate government funds to some more relevant major projects and programs in order to achieve the mandate of the agency and for the good of government service.

Direct the municipal accountant to support the disbursement vouchers with relevant information and documentary requirements to prove validity and legality of transactions.
observations.managementAction: As for the deficiency of some supporting documents, deviations noted will be addressed immediately and will not occur in the near future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to be more judicious and adhere to existing guidelines in the utilization of SEF funds.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to execute a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) to be agreed by both the LGU and the recipient before the release of fund. Direct the Municipal Accountant to support adequate documentary requirements on fund transfers to NGO/PO as required by COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012. Also, the Municipal Treasurer should be prudent in naming a check to proper claimant before actual release of check.
observations.managementAction: Adapted the suggestion manifested in this finding as to the handling of related transactions in the future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE), being the Head of the Procuring Entity (HoPE), to ensure that the minimum requirement of the BAC composition be met before the conduct of procurement activities. Moreover, we recommend that the HoPE will appoint or delegate alternate members so that BAC procurement activities will not be disrupted.
observations.managementAction: We recommended to the Head of Procuring Entity the appointment of alternative BAC members or at least see to it that a minimum number of members will be met.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Administration of LGU-Pangantucan, Bukidnon will address its absorption capacity to undertake infrastructure projects.  Look into manpower constraints and review the benefits of infra projects being implemented by administration vis-a-vis by contract.
observations.managementAction: The administration already engaged on the straight contract mode of procurement. Already has the initiative to hire additional engineers indispensable to the fact that we lack manpower complement in the engineering office.
observations.reason: Lack of heavy equipment to undertake the programmed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant, municipal budget officer and the peace and order council of the municipality to be more discrete and limit cash advance of confidential fund for three (3) months requirement.  Adhere strictly to the provisions of Joint Circular 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Adherence to the mentioned guidelines will be strictly implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the LGU to stop the release of mid-year, year-end and cash gift bonuses earlier than what is required by rules and regulations and require the Municipal Accountant to withhold taxes on personnel who receives bonuses and benefits that exceeded the P90,000.00 threshold.

Please be informed further that under Section 10 of Budget Circular No. 2016-4 and Section 9.0 of Budget Circular No. 2017-2 provides that the responsible officers shall be held liable for any payment of Year-end and Mid-Year Bonus not in accordance with the provisions of this Circular, without prejudice to the refund by the employees concerned of any excess or unauthorized payments.
observations.managementAction: Follow diligently onwards such mechanism to eradicate this erroneous processes.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Assessor to follow up the status of the submitted documents (for titling of the real properties of the municipality) at the Registry of Deeds and DENR provincial office at Malaybalay City to ensure titling of all the lands owned by the municipality in compliance to Section 47 (C) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.

Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements information on these real properties with DAR MOA agreement.
observations.reason: Application of special patents for lands under Presidential Proclamation are made and are still in process.

observations.recommendation: Request the Local Chief Executive to create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all local roads and prepare Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System. Direct the GSO to maintain a Local Road Inventory, Road Map, Local Road Network Property Card, and prepare Report on Local Road Network for all roads and its components annually. Then, instruct the Municipal Engineer to render complete description and cost segregation of all road components, serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Voucher to record the costing of local road network in the general ledger and in the Local Road Network Ledger Card.
observations.reason: Cost of local road networks were booked to the books of accounts. However, the Inventory Committee was not reconstituted during the year. Municipal Engineer failed to render cost segregation of all road components serving as basis for the Municipal Accountant to record the costing of local road networks.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Agriculturist to send another billing statement (2nd Tracer) to the concerned farmers to settle their obligations to the LGU. To direct the concerned office to confer with the farmers on the cause on nonpayment and possible solutions to avoid the effect of the penal provisions provided in the IRR and the loan contract. Identify the allowable expenditure out of the collections from the loans.
observations.reason: The concerned offices already sent statement of account to each beneficiary for the settlement of their obligations. However, still failed to provide clear-cut provisions on the use of loan collections.

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the COA audit team all perfected contracts, along with its related documents, in earliest time possible and examine carefully the completeness of the required documents before submission to avoid delay in the legal and auditorial review and the technical evaluation thereof, as explicitly provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

Expedite the disposal of all unserviceable and obsolete properties of the municipality to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from their disposal. Specifically, Management should create committees to undertake the appraisal and disposal of these properties in accordance to COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989 and PD 1445. Moreover, a request for audit action should be submitted to COA audit team before the conduct of proper disposal activity. Furthermore, the reports prepared should be furnished to the Municipal Accountant who shall prepare journal vouchers to drop the properties from the books of account.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was not reconstituted during the year. Failed to dispose unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to: 

a) Retrace the pertinent disbursement vouchers/JEVs relating to the deducted retentions from 2009 and prior years, the review, analyzed and reconciled with other related accounts in the trial balance, and determine their existence and validity;
b) Effect the adjusting journal entries;
c)    Determine proper disposition of reconciled and validated accounts as follows:
-Settlement of all Guaranty deposits payable and/or reversion/adjustment of the same to the proper account.
d)  If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to absence of records and documents, the Municipal Mayor should request for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances from the COA, supported by:
-List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts; and,
-Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules and supporting vouchers/documents cannot be located.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a)  Adhere to the policy of determining eligibility of bidders in a competitive bidding and require suppliers to submit the mandatory documentary requirements for alternative mode of procurement;
b)  Stop the practice of requiring the prospective bidder to pay and apply for a “bidder’s permit” prior to participation in a public bidding; and,
c)  Adhere to the provision to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report every semester and submit the same to the GPPB.

Require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the COA audit team all perfected contracts, along with its related documents, in earliest time possible and examine carefully the completeness of the required documents before submission to avoid delay in the legal and auditorial review and the technical evaluation thereof, as explicitly provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.reason: Some contracts were not submitted to COA within the prescribed period due to failure to return some of the documents by the winning bidder on a specified date.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Accountant to take up the fund in the proper book of account and to observe the posting and reporting requirement on the utilization of the LGSF.

Submit the rest of the disbursement vouchers and the copy of the Memorandum of Agreement to prove propriety of the disbursements from the LGSF made during the year.

Require the Local Development Committee to prioritize the development projects that are workable and attainable to avoid delays in the implementation and completion, thus attaining socio-economic development and environment outcome in an efficient and effective manner.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to exert extra effort to uncover reconciling item(s) of the affected reciprocal accounts and effect necessary adjusting entries to ascertain accuracy and fair presentation of separate statement of financial condition of each fund.

Adjust and add back the sum of P4,198,122.84 to the Total Continuing Appropriations for the year and closely observe the provision stated in Section 322 of Republic Act No. 7160.

Communicate and coordinate closely with the Bureau of Treasury and some other national agencies if funds are to be downloaded or already transferred in favor to the accounts of the municipality. Further, it is highly recommended that the latter will enroll its bank accounts to LandBank’s WeAccess online facility for easy retrieval, monitoring and reconciliation of bank transactions, especially in faster detection of fund/s received from whatever source that need immediate recording and implementation.

Prepare PPMP for all programs and/or projects including those funded by the 20% LDF.  The preparation of PPMPs is consistent with the requirement of the procurement law and that of the Government Procurement Policy Board-Technical Support Office (GPPB-TSO) which should be observed and implemented by all government agencies.  Unless PPMPs are formulated, actual procurement may result to either non-implementation or delay, in the absence of the pertinent procurement schedules and inexact terms of technical specifications, scope of work, or terms of reference specifically needed by the end-user or implementing units.

It is recommended that Management should require the Municipal Accounting Office to restore the corresponding amount of cash advance. The Municipal Accountant shall record the liquidation of the cash advance in the books of accounts through journal entry voucher only based on a copy of the duly received transmittal letter by ICFAU evidencing receipt of liquidation report. Adherence to the aforementioned regulation should be strictly observed, particularly on the liquidation process to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advance appearing in the municipality’s financial reports.

It is recommended that Management should require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters addressed to the aforesaid NGOs/POs and NGA requiring them to immediately submit liquidation reports of their spending of LGU funds.

It is recommended that Management should require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare on a monthly basis the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of the month through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council to the COA Auditor in compliance with the reportorial requirements of the afore-cited COA Circular.

It is recommended that Management should require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit and compile all the necessary documentary requirements such as the individual program of works (POW), especially on projects regarding concreting of municipal roads as dictated in COA Circular 2012-001.

It is recommended that Management should require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to conduct a mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions, specifications and cost estimates in the bidding documents after declaration of first bidding failure, so as not to defeat Section 35 of Revised IRR of Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184).

observations.managementAction: Project implementation started already in CY 2016. As of December 31, 2016, two (2) out of three (3) DILG-funded projects have already been completed.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that Management should assure that all projects/programs/activities appropriated out of 20% Development Fund be fully prioritized for utilization and implementation, as to provide the constituents the maximum and immediate benefits that can be derived therefrom. Likewise, adherence to the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM dated April 13, 2011 pertaining to the programs/projects funded under 20% Development Fund should be strictly observed.
